title: Eleocharis tumida

It belongs to the genus Eleocharis, which includes over 200 species of sedges. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but Eleocharis tumida is generally considered to be a type of true sedge. This plant is native to certain regions and is often found in wetland environments. The classification of Eleocharis tumida is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, which is used to identify and classify species in the biological world.

intro: Eleocharis tumida is a perennial, wetland plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae), commonly found in marshy and aquatic environments. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
